text: Git branching is a powerful feature in version control that helps teams work on code without interfering with each other. Developers create feature branches to work on new features or bug fixes separately from the main codebase. Once the work is complete, they can merge their branch back into the main branch. Before merging, teams often use pull requests to review changes, discuss improvements, and ensure code quality. This workflow keeps the main branch stable and organized. Understanding this process is essential for efficient collaboration. With practice, you will be able to manage complex projects smoothly and avoid common version control mistakes.
